title: Named-effect routing pilot (beyond cognitive biases)
scope: A deliberately bounded two-item pilot pairing selected by PLAN P2-E to test evidence routing across different evidence types: a laboratory social-psychology emotion effect (misattribution of arousal) and a contested field criminology/social-order effect (broken windows). It is NOT a taxonomy or an exhaustive list of non-cognitive-bias effects; the Dunning-Kruger effect, the third named pilot, is already registered under the cognitive-bias discovery seed. Registration records named identities only and asserts nothing about reality, replicability, or causal evidence.
notes: Second named-phenomenon slice (P1-E). A discovery seed can only produce candidates; it cannot support an evidence verdict. These two pilots plus the already-registered Dunning-Kruger effect are the P2-E source-routing pilots.
